settings.saved_language_note: Saved. Restart the window to apply the new language.
monitor.armed_confirm_text: This will actually lock the screen when the trusted device leaves the safety radius. Continue?
calibration.tab_intro: Calibrate distance estimation for the trusted device by sampling RSSI at two known distances.
calibration.no_device_text: Pick a trusted device on the Device tab first.
calibration.wizard_step: Step {step} of {total}: stand at {distance:g} m from this computer with the device where you normally carry it, then press Sample.
calibration.station_skipped: Only {count} advertisement(s) received at {distance:g} m - device not advertising or out of range. This station will be skipped.
calibration.station_ok: Median RSSI at {distance:g} m: {median:.0f} dBm ({count} samples).
calibration.fit_no_samples: Calibration failed: no usable samples were collected. Move closer to the device or check that Bluetooth is on, then try again.
calibration.fit_ok: Calibrated: rssi_at_1m={rssi:.1f} dBm, path loss exponent n={exponent:.2f}
caveat.classic_link_windows: classic_link on Windows reports reachability only (connected / out of range), not a metric distance. The radius slider has no effect here; the lock fires on disconnect, similar to Windows Dynamic Lock.
caveat.classic_link_other: classic_link reports real RSSI on this platform, so the radius slider behaves as a metric threshold, same as adv_scan.
